pi_items.source: File exists at examples/equest_env/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp, confirmed as DOE-2 format building input file with project title 'Project 18'
Grep search found 5 middle floor systems: M.S21, M.E22, M.N23, M.W24, M.C25 all matching pattern
ASHRAE Standard 55 and technical guidance recommend relative humidity range of 30-60% for occupant comfort and health. This is the lower bound of the recommended range.
ASHRAE Standard 55 and technical guidance recommend relative humidity range of 30-60% for occupant comfort and health. This is the upper bound of the recommended range.
eQUEST/DOE-2 documentation and user forums confirm HUMIDIFIER-TYPE parameter accepts 'ELECTRIC' as valid value. This is the standard humidifier type available in eQUEST.
Task description requires 'run the full annual DOE-2.2 simulation' after modifying systems. Verifier checks sim_file_is_new flag and passes only if simulation ran.
Grep search on 4StoreyBuilding.inp file: lines 2807, 2848, 2889, 2930, 2971 define the 5 middle floor PSZ systems with these exact IDs

privileged_info_summary: The task targets a synthetic 4StoreyBuilding model with 5 middle-floor PSZ HVAC systems. The actual system identifiers in the model are M.S21, M.E22, M.N23, M.W24, and M.C25, NOT M.S16, M.E17, M.N18, M.W19, M.C20 as stated in the task description. The expected humidity setpoints (MIN=30%, MAX=60%) align with ASHRAE Standard 55 recommendations for occupant comfort. The HUMIDIFIER-TYPE=ELECTRIC is a valid DOE-2 parameter supported by eQUEST.
